Journal.Title: Zhonghua jie he he hu xi za zhi = Zhonghua jiehe he huxi zazhi = Chinese journal of tuberculosis and respiratory diseases
Journal.ISOAbbreviation: Zhonghua Jie He He Hu Xi Za Zhi

Article.ArticleTitle: [Characteristics and regional reference standards of peak expiratory flow in children and adolescents in high-altitude regions of Xizang, China: a cross-sectional study].

Abstract.AbstractText: Objective: To systematically evaluate the applicability of existing peak expiratory flow (PEF) prediction equations from plain area for children and adolescents in high-altitude regions of Xizang, and to establish PEF reference percentiles and prediction regression equations characteristic of high-altitude populations. Methods: A cross-sectional survey was conducted from September to October 2024 in 17 primary and secondary schools in Xizang, covering altitudes of 3 200 to 4 500 m. PEF was measured using a COSMED Micro Quark portable spirometer. Agreement between measured PEF values and those predicted by low-altitude equations was assessed using Bland-Altman analysis. Age-and sex-specific PEF reference percentiles were constructed using generalized additive models for location, scale and shape (GAMLSS), and local prediction regression equations were developed. Results: A total of 3 427 healthy children and adolescents in Xizang aged 7-17 years were included (1 567 boys and 1 860 girls). Plain-area prediction equations generally overestimated PEF in the pediatric population of Xizang. PEF reference percentiles were established based on the Box-Cox power exponential (BCPE) distribution, and local prediction equations were developed with height, weight, and residential altitude (or age) as the main explanatory variables. Conclusions: This study is the first to establish PEF reference percentiles and prediction equations for children on the Xizang plateau, clearly demonstrating the limitations of plain-area prediction equations in high-altitude populations. These findings provide a scientific basis for pediatric respiratory health screening, disease management, and public health policymaking in high-altitude regions.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: School of Social Sports, Tianjin University of Sport, Tianjin 301617, China.
Tianjin Citizens' Scientific Fitness Service Center,Tianjin 300061,China.
Department of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine, Tianjin Chest Hospital, Tianjin 300222, China.
School of Social Sports, Tianjin University of Sport, Tianjin 301617, China.
School of Physical Education, Qinghai Minzu University, Xining 810007, China.

Grant.Agency: Major Research Project of the Xizang Autonomous Region Institute of Educational Sciences

MedlineJournalInfo.MedlineTA: Zhonghua Jie He He Hu Xi Za Zhi

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 目的： 系统评估现有平原地区呼气流量峰值（PEF）预测方程在西藏高海拔儿童青少年群体中的适用性，并建立符合高原人群特征的PEF参考百分位数及预测回归方程。 方法： 于2024年9—10月在西藏17所中小学开展横断面调查，覆盖海拔3 200~4 500 m。采用COSMED Micro Quark便携式肺功能仪测定PEF。比较实测值与平原地区预测方程预测值的一致性，采用Bland-Altman分析评估偏倚特征；基于位置-尺度-形状广义加性模型（GAMLSS）构建分年龄、分性别的PEF百分位参考值，并建立本地预测回归方程。 结果： 共纳入7~17岁健康藏族儿童青少年3 427人（男1 567人，女1 860人）。平原预测方程在西藏儿童群体中普遍高估PEF。基于BCPE分布建立了PEF百分位参考值，并构建了以身高、体重及居住海拔（或年龄）为主要解释变量的本地预测方程。 结论： 本研究首次在西藏高原儿童群体中建立了PEF百分位参考标准及预测方程，明确了平原预测方程在高原人群中的局限性。研究结果可为高原地区儿童呼吸健康筛查、疾病管理及公共卫生政策制定提供科学依据。.
